description_long: This object consists of four long, single-edged swords with slightly curved blades and straight handles. Each sword features a distinct blade color, including green, black, and reddish hues, and all have a metallic sheen. The handles are cylindrical, appearing to be made of wood or a wood-like material, with small spherical pommels at the end. Each sword is equipped with a simple, round guard separating the blade from the handle, providing basic hand protection. The blades are broad and taper to a sharp point, with a pronounced angle at the tip, reminiscent of a Chinese dao or machete. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function over decoration.
description: A set of four long, single-edged swords with curved blades, straight wooden handles, and simple round guards. The blades are colored green, black, and reddish, with a metallic finish.
description_view_0: The swords are viewed edge-on, showing the thinness of the blades and the alignment of the guards and handles.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the full width and curvature of the blades, as well as the color differences and the round guards.
description_view_2: The swords are again viewed edge-on from the opposite side, highlighting the blade thickness and the cylindrical handles.
description_view_3: A rear angled view showing the backs of the blades, the pommels, and the subtle color variations on the handles.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical wood, small spherical pommel, no visible grip texture
